turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Enquiring About a Rental Property or Housing Inspection

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, I’m calling about an apartment I saw online. I just want to ask some questions because I’m not very familiar with rental process in Australia.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Sure, that’s no problem at all. Which property are you enquiring about?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s a one-bedroom apartment near Central Station. I think it was posted maybe yesterday or two days ago.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, I can see that listing. Are you calling about inspection times or the application process?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Inspection first. Because I don’t want to apply before I see inside.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That makes sense. We have an inspection scheduled this Saturday at 11am.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Saturday 11am… Sorry, is that fixed time, or can I come earlier or later?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: The inspection runs for about 30 minutes, so you can arrive anytime between 11 and .

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Ah okay. So not exactly 11, but within that time.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Exactly.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, good. Last time I went too late and missed it, so I wanted to confirm.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s understandable. You’ll also need to register online before attending.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Register… Do you mean like make an account?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Not a full account, just your name and email address.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, that’s simple. And after register, I get email?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, you’ll receive a confirmation email.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Another question, sorry.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s fine, go ahead.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Is the apartment furnished or unfurnished? Because photos sometimes confusing.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: It’s unfurnished.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Unfurnished meaning no bed, no fridge, no washing machine?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: There’s no furniture, but the kitchen appliances are included.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Ah, okay. So fridge yes, but bed no.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s right.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Good, thanks for clarifying. And about bond, how much is it?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: The bond is four weeks’ rent.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Four weeks… Rent is 520 per week, so bond is… Sorry, I need a moment.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That would be 2,080 dollars.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Ah okay. Yes, now it’s clear.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Do you have any other questions?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, one more. I’m international student and I don’t have rental history in Australia. Is that a big disadvantage?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Not necessarily. Many students apply without rental history.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, that’s good to hear. I was worried about that.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You can include proof of income or a guarantor if needed.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Guarantor meaning someone responsible if I don’t pay?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, exactly.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, I understand now. Thank you for explaining everything.
